conclusion: Adding intermediate layer limits changes the c4 rowchunk picture: rowchunk2 is still generated-token green and has no full-attention stage failures through L4-L8, but its final hidden oracle is over tolerance at L5 and L7 and recovers at L6/L8. Rowchunk3 inherits the L5 hidden drift and becomes token-red under the layer-limit-6 truncated oracle (row1 first mismatch at generated index 4), while rowchunk3-specific linear projection drift starts at layer4 QKV/Z for rows0..2 and the later L8 full-attention trace still first fails at layer7 attn_input_pre_qkv. The grouped>=3 issue is therefore observable before the second full-attention layer in layer-limited trajectories, with rowchunk2 remaining the generated-token-green control.
